right hepatic artery, which was pretty big and traveling exactly anterior to the bile duct.
Usually we see hepatic artery, whenever present, it travels anterior to CHD, not to CBD.

this is here the artery was traveling right in front of a bile duct and this was the case of bile duct exploration that we have taken for the surgery so it was a little tricky situation to handle one can see the posteriorly right hepatic artery traveling within the calyx area so once we have seen the anatomy we gradually started dissecting the
hepatic artery this is the lymph node along with the gallbladder that's a hallmark and landmark for the dissection of gallbladder and
we tried to separate the artery away from the bile duct so that we can get the proper area for the exploration of the CBD to retrieve the stones.
So we followed the course of hepatic artery to identify the cystic, which was found expectedly just below the lymph node.
So with the help of sharp dissection with the monopolar hook and as and when required we use the bipolar diathermy to delineate and exactly identify the all separate vessels.
The anterior peritoneum over the gallbladder area is now separated.
small buzz of short current is used so that the things are cauterized properly and won't lead to significant amount of bleed even if the small branches of vessel is cauterized.
One can now see the dissection of lymph node as well as the anterior cystic artery.
So that's the anterior cystic artery getting dissected.
Careful dissection is carried 360 degree around the vessel.
The vessel is grasped with an atraumatic grasper and 360 degree space is created along with the vessel.
It is safeguarded and clipped just over the gallbladder.
Further dissection is carried to identify the cystic duct.
Safe window is now created.

Meticulous use of monopolar diathermy can lead to proper opening of the tissue planes and all the things can be easily dissected off with the help of monopolar diathermy in the cholecystectomy surgery.
Once we were secure, we divided the cystic from its distal end.
further careful dissection is carried forward on the body of gallbladder.
The best part of cholecystectomy surgery in this particular variety is to remain close to the gallbladder rather than going anywhere away.
Cystic duct is

completely dissected and a clip is applied at the distal bone part of cystic duct where it is joining the common bile duct and one can still see a large part of artery coming into the way of dissection of CBD.
So we further decided to dissect the artery so that it can be reflected easily from our way of exploration of the bile duct.
We chase the artery till it's entering the liver from behind and we clip the posterior cystic artery when it is entering into the gallbladder.
Distill end of artery is then divided with the monopolar diathermy.
You can now see the completely dissected anterior and posterior cystic arteries.
And the right hepatic artery is traveling well through the callus triangle to the liver area.
Despite our complete dissection up to the hilar area of gallbladder, the artery was still not getting reflected by its own.
So we decided to retract the artery with the help of a fine suture.

so that steady state of retraction is maintained all throughout peri arterial peritoneal tissue is then grasped and suture is tight so that the artery can be retracted medially that's on table modification or improvisation one can say to retract the artery constantly
taking it away from the surgical site exploration of bile duct.

the posterior medial aspect of arteries further dissected and the bile duct can be brought into the vision after the confirmation of the biliary aspiration and palpation of the stone along with the stent and incision is kept over the stone itself on the common bile duct as it was more than 1.5 centimeter size of
bile duct we decided to cut open with the horizontal incision which is in a way easier to close later on.
The large discoid shaped stone is then delivered.
Another large stone was found in the proximal CHD which was
brought into the vision and delivered with the help of forceps and pressure.
Both the stones were completely cleared from the bile duct.

The biliary is inspected.

view and the endobiliary stent which was visible.
Medial and distal margins of bile duct is then sutured horizontally itself with 2-0 Vicryl multiple interrupted sutures.
This could have not been possible unless we could have retracted the artery with the suture.
So that made our life very easy and kept the artery constantly away from our vision as well as the area of CBD exploration.
Once the proximal end was secured the distal end was secured with the sutures over the bile duct.
We are using 2-0 Vicryl for the same, but in case of thin bile duct or hepatic vaginostomy kind of a surgery, we use usually 4-0 or 5-0 Vicryl or PDS suture.
But in case of thick dilated CBD, we use 2-0 Vicryl.
That is our practice to use 2-0 Vicryl even in presence of Merezi syndrome as well.
and the last few sutures are taken with the care so that complete pile duct is closed few sutures are pre placed and then
Final switches are taken.
The cystic duct is clipped.
Cholecystectomy is always completed in the last so that we can maintain the steady retraction during the CBD exploration as well as the closure of bile duct.
Gradually the gallbladder is separated from liver bed and the entire specimen is then delivered via a specimen retrieval bag.
Wash is given in the area.
All the stumps and suture line are inspected thoroughly for any leakage or any bleeding.
A Jackson Pratt drain is kept and the specimen is delivered via umbilical port.
